notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix â†’ /nw-deliver. The authoritative specification is the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-mixed-backend-dispatch-spin/deliver/bugfix-rca.md` (RCA validated by @nw-troubleshooter 5-Whys evidence-cited; solution researched at `docs/research/reconcilers/dispatch-boundary-validation-and-attempt-budget-backoff.md`, High confidence). The defect: a service with BOTH local and remote backends makes `ServiceMapHydrator::reconcile` emit `DataplaneUpdateService(vip)` (XDP route) + `RegisterLocalBackend(vip, port)` (cgroup route) for one VIP in a single tick; the validator's cross-route arms reject this legitimate ADR-0053 Â§4 dual-path as a conflict, dispatch is skipped, no `ServiceHydrationStatus` row is ever written, and `should_dispatch`'s unconditional `None | Pending => true` arm re-emits the conflicting pair every tick with no backoff â€” an unbounded tight loop.

TWO independent root causes, THREE fixes. Cause A (primary, design-contradiction): the validator's cross-route VIP-level rejection contradicts already-accepted ADR-0053 Â§2/Â§4/Â§5 (mixed-backend dual-path is correct; XDP serves remote, cgroup serves local, disjoint kernel maps, no precedence race). Cause B (independent contributor): `should_dispatch` applies the backoff window ONLY on the `Failed` arm, so when dispatch is suppressed the `None | Pending => true` arm spins. Fix A1 narrows the validator to `(route, key-tuple)` granularity; Fix B lifts the backoff gate into the `None | Pending` arm; Fix C escalates the surviving genuine-conflict signal from log-only to a queryable observation row (user decision 2026-06-03).

Per-step Outside-In TDD: each step ships its regression test RED FIRST, then the fix, with the crafter running the 3-phase DES canon (RED â†’ GREEN â†’ COMMIT) internally within the single step. Step IDs match `^\d{2}-\d{2}$`. Sequencing: 01-01 (Fix A1, the core fix that unblocks the spin) â†’ 01-02 (Fix B, the attempt-budget gate, depends on nothing but pairs with A1's no-spin guarantee) â†’ 01-03 (Fix C type + envelope + golden fixture in overdrive-core) â†’ 01-04 (Fix C run_convergence_tick write-path wiring + regression test in overdrive-control-plane).

DECOMPOSITION DECISION â€” Fix C is SPLIT into 01-03 + 01-04. Rationale: Fix C crosses two distinct surfaces in two different crates with two different disciplines. (1) 01-03 lands the new observation-row type + `VersionedEnvelope` enum + `Latest`/payload aliases + discriminant-offset const + golden-bytes fixture, all in `overdrive-core`, governed by the rkyv schema-evolution single-commit discipline (`.claude/rules/development.md` Â§ "rkyv schema evolution" â†’ "Version-bump procedure" â€” type and fixture land atomically). (2) 01-04 wires `run_convergence_tick` to WRITE that row on validator `Err`, in `overdrive-control-plane`, governed by the surface-then-continue self-heal posture. Keeping these as one step would bundle a schema-evolution landing (overdrive-core, fixture-pinned) with a runtime write-path landing (overdrive-control-plane, ObservationStore-write) â€” two crates, two test entrypoints, two review concerns. The split keeps each step's file-count and review surface coherent and matches the schema-evolution discipline that the envelope + its golden fixture land in one atomic commit, independent of the consumer wiring.

RED strategy per step. 01-01 (validator): the existing tests `validate_rejects_xdp_then_cgroup_for_same_vip` and `validate_rejects_cgroup_then_xdp_for_same_vip` currently ASSERT rejection of the dual-path â€” they pass against current (bugged) code. The RED state is the NEW acceptance test `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` (asserts the dual-path is ACCEPTED) which FAILS against current code (the validator rejects it). The crafter writes that test, watches it fail, then lands the logic + doc change which flips it GREEN AND flips the two cross-route rejection tests into the renamed acceptance test (deletion/test discipline: rename `validate_rejects_*_for_same_vip` â†’ `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip`, delete the now-wrong assertions). 01-02 (should_dispatch): NEW unit test `mixed_backend_does_not_re_dispatch_within_backoff_window_with_no_status_row` fails against current code (the `None | Pending => true` arm re-dispatches every tick). 01-04 (Fix C write-path): NEW acceptance test `genuine_same_slot_conflict_produces_queryable_observation_row` fails against current code (no row is written, only a tracing event).

Scope discipline (CRITICAL). This roadmap covers EXACTLY the three fixes in the RCA Â§Solution. Do NOT add steps or scope for: progressive/exponential backoff (`backoff_for_attempt` stays constant 1s; promotion tracked at GH #137); full wiring of hydrator `RetryMemory` into the runtime re-enqueue predicate (GH #160 â€” touch only if Fix B strictly requires it, otherwise leave to #160); IPv6 VIP conflict class (GH #155). The ADR-0053 revision 2026-06-03 + D11 evolution-doc scope clarification are ALREADY LANDED by the architect (uncommitted in working tree at RCA time) â€” do NOT add a doc-edit step for them. If the crafter discovers a needed deferral beyond these three tracked items, surface it to the user for approval â€” do NOT run `gh issue create` (per CLAUDE.md Â§ "Deferrals require GitHub issues â€” AND user approval BEFORE creation").

Fix A1 verbatim doc prose. The validator's module-doc header (`//! Reconcile-output invariant validator.` through the end of the "Ordered-collection choice" paragraph) is REPLACED VERBATIM with the architect-supplied prose at `docs/feature/fix-mixed-backend-dispatch-spin/deliver/architect-validate-doc-prose.md`. The crafter owns the LOGIC change (delete the two cross-route match arms + the `cgroup_vips` tracker; widen `xdp_vips` â†’ a `(vip, port, proto)` slot set and keep `cgroup_keys` as `(vip, vip_port, proto)`); the doc block is applied verbatim, with the one documented amendment: when Fix C lands (01-04) the "Fail-safe semantics" paragraph is extended to name the observation row as the queryable surface alongside the tracing event (per the architect doc's `## Note on Fix C`).

Fix B honors "Persist inputs, not derived state" (`.claude/rules/development.md`). The `None | Pending` arm consults ONLY inputs `RetryMemory` already persists (`attempts`, `last_failure_seen_at`, `last_attempted_fingerprint`). NO `next_attempt_at` field is introduced â€” the deadline is recomputed every tick as `last_failure_seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(attempts)`. The `Failed`-arm gate is lifted into a shared helper (e.g. `backoff_window_elapsed(retry, now)`) so the `None | Pending` arm and the `Failed` arm apply identical window semantics. On `desired_fingerprint != last_attempted_fingerprint` the arm dispatches immediately (level-triggered reset on spec change, per the client-go workqueue precedent in the RCA). Reconciler purity is preserved: `should_dispatch` is pure over its inputs and uses the `now: UnixInstant` parameter only â€” never `Instant::now()` / `SystemTime::now()`; `cargo xtask dst-lint` enforces this.

Fix C crosses the rkyv schema-evolution boundary (`.claude/rules/development.md` Â§ "rkyv schema evolution" + `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ "Archive schema-evolution roundtrip"). The new conflict observation row gets: a payload struct (`...V1`), a `VersionedEnvelope` enum (`...Envelope` with a single `V1` variant), `Latest`/payload `pub type` aliases, a `discriminant_offset_from_end()` const, `known_discriminants()`, and `type_name()` â€” ALL mirroring `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope` (`observation_store.rs:782-863`). A golden-bytes fixture file lands under `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/<row>.rs`, registered in the `mod schema_evolution { ... }` block of `tests/schema_evolution.rs`, mirroring `service_hydration_result_row.rs` (the `print_fixture_v<N>_bytes` regeneration aid, the triangulation test, the unknown-version probe test). The envelope + the golden fixture land in ONE atomic commit per the Version-bump procedure (this is the V1 mint, not a bump â€” but the same atomicity discipline applies). FIXTURE_V1 is generated once via the `#[ignore]`d regeneration test and pinned verbatim.

Fix C posture â€” surface-then-continue, NO hard stop. `run_convergence_tick`'s existing `if let Err(violation)` arm (`reconciler_runtime.rs:1140-1149`) KEEPS the `tracing::error!` `reconciler.output.invariant_violation` event (Kubernetes Events model: best-effort human signal) AND ADDS a write of the new conflict observation row through `state.obs` (the machine-queryable control signal). Do NOT import controller-runtime `TerminalError` stop-semantics. Do NOT skip the View persist. Convergence retries next tick. The appliance OS has no operator shell (`.claude/rules/reconcilers.md`: "the system must self-heal").

Test lanes. 01-01 tests are pure unit-shaped over `validate_reconcile_output` typed inputs â€” default nextest lane, in the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block of `validate.rs`. 01-02 tests are pure unit-shaped over `should_dispatch` typed inputs â€” default lane, in the existing `#[cfg(test)]` block of `service_map_hydrator.rs`. 01-03 schema-evolution tests are pure in-memory rkyv roundtrips â€” default lane, under `tests/schema_evolution/`. 01-04's regression test exercises `run_convergence_tick` against sim adapters (`SimObservationStore`, `SimClock`, `LocalIntentStore` on a TempDir) â€” default acceptance lane in `overdrive-control-plane` (the acceptance suite in this crate is NOT behind `integration-tests` per its entrypoint header). NONE of these steps require `--features integration-tests` or Lima for the regression tests themselves; the macOS `--no-run` integration typecheck + Lima full run remain part of each step's quality gate to catch cross-crate compile drift.

Mutation gate per step (post-GREEN, `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ "Mutation testing is the exception"). 01-01: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/validate.rs` (no `--features integration-tests` â€” validator tests are default-lane). 01-02 + 01-03: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file <file>`. 01-04: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs --features integration-tests` (run through `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS if integration tests participate). Kill-rate gate â‰Ą80%. On a surviving mutation, add a disambiguating test case â€” never weaken an assertion. cargo-mutants restores the tree itself; do NOT run `git checkout -- crates/` after the pass.

Standing user-rule references the crafter must obey across all four steps: (1) `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` â€” no shims, no deprecations; old + new land in the same commit (the renamed validator tests, the `cgroup_vips` tracker removal, the doc replacement all land in 01-01's single commit). (2) `feedback_no_git_stash` â€” focused-subset commits use `git add <paths>` + `git restore --staged`, never `git stash` (note: this overrides the repo CLAUDE.md's stash-pattern guidance per the user's standing feedback). (3) `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` â€” clippy `-D warnings` issues surfaced during the work get fixed in-scope, never classified as pre-existing. (4) `feedback_delete_dont_gate` â€” deleting the cross-route arms means deleting their tests, not gating them. (5) `feedback_red_scaffold_should_panic` â€” where a RED scaffold is needed, use `#[should_panic(expected = "RED scaffold")]`; here the RED state is a real failing assertion against current code (no scaffold attribute needed), so the crafter watches the new test FAIL, then lands the fix to flip it GREEN within the same step's commit.

Roadmap-validator length warnings (`STEP_NAME_TOO_LONG`, `CRITERIA_TOO_LONG`, `DESCRIPTION_TOO_LONG`) are cosmetic and non-blocking per CLAUDE.md Â§ "Roadmap validator warnings" â€” Overdrive ACs deliberately carry scenario-level specificity (test fn names, the `(vip, port, proto)` tuple, invariant names, golden-fixture requirements). Do NOT trim ACs to silence these warnings.

phases.name: Fix the mixed-backend dispatch spin â€” narrow the validator to (route, key-tuple), gate should_dispatch on the attempt budget, escalate genuine conflicts to a queryable observation row
phases.description: Four-step Outside-In TDD bugfix covering the THREE fixes in the RCA Â§Solution, each step running RED â†’ GREEN â†’ COMMIT internally. 01-01 (Fix A1): narrow `validate_reconcile_output` from VIP-level cross-route rejection to `(route, key-tuple)` granularity â€” delete the two cross-route match arms + the `cgroup_vips` tracker, widen `xdp_vips` â†’ a `(vip, port, proto)` slot set, apply the architect's verbatim module-doc, flip the two cross-route rejection tests to one acceptance test, extend same-route tests across the proto dimension. 01-02 (Fix B): lift the `Failed`-arm backoff gate into a shared helper and apply it in the `None | Pending` arm of `should_dispatch` (window applied when prior attempts at the same `last_attempted_fingerprint`; immediate dispatch on fingerprint change), persisting inputs not derived state. 01-03 (Fix C, part 1): mint the new conflict observation-row type + `VersionedEnvelope` + aliases + discriminant-offset const + golden-bytes fixture in `overdrive-core`, mirroring `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope`. 01-04 (Fix C, part 2): wire `run_convergence_tick` to write that row on validator `Err` alongside the existing tracing event (surface-then-continue, no stop), with the regression test asserting a genuine same-slot conflict produces the queryable row AND still skips dispatch. Out of scope (tracked, do NOT add steps): exponential backoff (#137), full RetryMemoryâ†’runtime re-enqueue (#160), IPv6 conflict class (#155).

steps.name: Fix A1 â€” narrow validate_reconcile_output to (route, key-tuple) granularity so the ADR-0053 Â§4 mixed-backend dual-path is accepted
steps.description: RED â†’ GREEN â†’ COMMIT in one step. Target: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/validate.rs`. RED: add a new `#[test] fn validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip()` in the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block asserting that `vec![update_service(1), register(vip_v4(1), 8080)]` (an XDP `DataplaneUpdateService` + a cgroup `RegisterLocalBackend` for the SAME VIP) returns `Ok(())` â€” this is the ADR-0053 Â§4 dual-path. Against current code it FAILS (the cross-route arm rejects it). Also add a `#[test] fn validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_distinct_proto_same_vip_port()` exercising the proto dimension: tcp/53 on the XDP route + udp/53 on the cgroup route for one VIP are distinct slots â†’ accepted. Watch both fail. GREEN â€” LOGIC change: (1) delete the two cross-route match arms (the `(WriteRoute::Xdp, _, _) if cgroup_vips.contains(&vip)` arm and the `(WriteRoute::Cgroup, _, _) if xdp_vips.contains(&vip)` arm); (2) delete the `cgroup_vips` tracker declaration and its `cgroup_vips.insert(vip)` write site; (3) widen `xdp_vips` (currently a VIP-only `BTreeSet<Ipv4Addr>` used only by the now-deleted cross-route check) â€” it is no longer needed for cross-route, so DELETE it entirely (the `xdp_keys: BTreeSet<(Ipv4Addr, u16, Proto)>` slot set already carries the full `(vip, port, proto)` granularity for same-route XDP-vs-XDP detection); (4) keep `cgroup_keys: BTreeSet<(Ipv4Addr, u16, Proto)>` as the `(vip, vip_port, proto)` slot set for same-route cgroup-vs-cgroup detection; (5) the remaining arms are exactly: XDP-vs-XDP same `(vip, port, proto)` â†’ reject; insert `(vip, port, proto)` into `xdp_keys`; cgroup-vs-cgroup same `(vip, vip_port, proto)` â†’ reject; insert into `cgroup_keys`. The `ConflictingServiceWrites` variant's `vip_port` is now ALWAYS `Some(port)` (no cross-route `None` case survives) â€” narrow the field's doc accordingly, or keep `Option<u16>` for forward-compat and document that Phase 1 always populates it (crafter's call per minimal-change discipline; prefer keeping `Option<u16>` to avoid churning the variant shape and downstream `matches!` sites). GREEN â€” DOC change: replace the module-doc header (`//! Reconcile-output invariant validator.` through the end of the "Ordered-collection choice" paragraph) VERBATIM with the architect prose at `docs/feature/fix-mixed-backend-dispatch-spin/deliver/architect-validate-doc-prose.md`. GREEN â€” TEST flip: rename `validate_rejects_xdp_then_cgroup_for_same_vip` and `validate_rejects_cgroup_then_xdp_for_same_vip` â€” delete both (their assertions are now wrong) and replace with the single `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` added in RED (deletion/test discipline per `feedback_delete_dont_gate`). KEEP the same-route same-slot rejection tests (`validate_rejects_two_xdp_writes_for_same_slot`, `validate_rejects_register_and_deregister_for_same_key`, `validate_rejects_cgroup_identical_vip_port_proto`) and the proto-dimension acceptance tests (`validate_accepts_xdp_same_vip_port_different_proto`, `validate_accepts_cgroup_same_vip_port_different_proto`). COMMIT: single cohesive commit, `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer, normal pre-commit hooks (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: `validate.rs` no longer rejects cross-route writes: `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` and `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_distinct_proto_same_vip_port` both pass (the dual-path returns `Ok(())`). The two cross-route match arms are deleted; the `cgroup_vips` and `xdp_vips` trackers are deleted (`git grep -n 'cgroup_vips\|xdp_vips' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/validate.rs` returns nothing). `xdp_keys` and `cgroup_keys` remain as `BTreeSet<(Ipv4Addr, u16, Proto)>` slot sets. The two `validate_rejects_*_for_same_vip` tests are deleted and replaced (not gated, not commented). Same-route same-slot rejection tests still pass (XDP-vs-XDP at one `(vip, port, proto)`; cgroup-vs-cgroup at one `(vip, vip_port, proto)`; register+deregister at one slot). Proto-dimension tests still pass. The module-doc header matches the architect prose verbatim. `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane --test acceptance` and the default lane are green; `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on overdrive-control-plane. Mutation gate: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/validate.rs` â‰Ą80% kill rate. Commit carries `Step-ID: 01-01`; no `--no-verify`.
acceptance_criteria: RCA Â§Root cause A (closure) â€” the validator's cross-route VIP-level rejection is removed; an XDP `DataplaneUpdateService(vip)` + cgroup `RegisterLocalBackend(vip, port)` in one tick is ACCEPTED per ADR-0053 Â§2/Â§4/Â§5. Pinned by `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` (RED against current code, GREEN after the fix).

RCA Â§Fix A1 â€” the same-route trackers are `(vip, port, proto)` (XDP, `xdp_keys`) and `(vip, vip_port, proto)` (cgroup, `cgroup_keys`); the `cgroup_vips` cross-route tracker and the now-orphaned `xdp_vips` tracker are deleted.
RCA Â§Regression test 1 (Mixed-backend accepted) â€” `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` is the load-bearing artifact; it fails against current main and passes after the fix.

RCA Â§Regression test 4 (Proto dimension) â€” tcp/53 + udp/53 on one VIP are distinct slots, accepted on EITHER route; pinned by `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_distinct_proto_same_vip_port` and the retained `validate_accepts_*_same_vip_port_different_proto` tests.

Genuine same-slot conflicts STILL rejected â€” two XDP writes to one `(vip, port, proto)` slot, two cgroup writes to one `(vip, vip_port, proto)` slot, and register+deregister at one slot all still return `Err(ConflictingServiceWrites)`. The surviving violation class is exactly what Fix C (01-04) will additionally observe.

The module-doc header is replaced VERBATIM with `docs/feature/fix-mixed-backend-dispatch-spin/deliver/architect-validate-doc-prose.md` (the `## Note on Fix C` amendment to the 'Fail-safe semantics' paragraph is applied in 01-04, not here).

Deletion/test discipline per `feedback_delete_dont_gate`: the two `validate_rejects_*_for_same_vip` tests are deleted and replaced with the acceptance test in the SAME commit â€” not `#[ignore]`d, not commented, not salvaged-and-rewritten.
Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: logic + doc + test flip all land in one commit; no shadow tracker, no two-phase rollout.
Reconciler/validator purity preserved â€” `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (the validator is pure over its `&[Action]` input; no clock, no I/O).

Mutation gate â‰Ą80% on `validate.rs`; the same-route rejection tests + the cross-route acceptance test jointly kill the 'reject-everything' / 'accept-everything' / 'flip the slot-equality' mutation classes.
Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (unit â€” default nextest lane, pure over `validate_reconcile_output(&[Action])` typed inputs, no I/O, no sim adapters, no feature flag). Mutation scope: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/validate.rs`.
quality_gates: RED verified at runtime BEFORE the logic change: `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` fails against current code with the validator returning `Err(ConflictingServiceWrites)` for the dual-path. Watch it fail, then land the fix.

After the fix: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` (default lane) green; the renamed acceptance test passes; the deleted `validate_rejects_*_for_same_vip` tests are gone (`git grep validate_rejects_xdp_then_cgroup` returns nothing).
`git grep -n 'cgroup_vips\|xdp_vips' crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/validate.rs` returns nothing â€” both cross-route trackers deleted.
Module-doc header matches the architect prose file verbatim (diff the header against `docs/feature/fix-mixed-backend-dispatch-spin/deliver/architect-validate-doc-prose.md` lines 10-101).

`cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck) exits 0; full integration run inside Lima VM (or LVH on CI) exits 0.
`cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on overdrive-control-plane.

Mutation gate (post-GREEN, `run_in_background: true`): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-control-plane --file crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/action_shim/validate.rs` reports â‰Ą80% kill rate. cargo-mutants restores the tree itself; do NOT run `git checkout -- crates/`.
Production edit precision: `git diff --name-only HEAD~1` shows exactly one file modified (`validate.rs`); no incidental edits to neighbouring action-shim files.
deliverables: Single cohesive commit landing the validator logic narrowing (delete two cross-route arms + `cgroup_vips` + orphaned `xdp_vips`; keep `(vip,port,proto)` / `(vip,vip_port,proto)` slot sets), the verbatim architect module-doc, and the test flip (two rejection tests â†’ one acceptance test; proto-dimension acceptance test added).
`validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` and `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_distinct_proto_same_vip_port` transition RED â†’ GREEN within this commit; same-route rejection tests stay GREEN.
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): this is a Rust workspace â€” unit tests are `#[test]` functions in the `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block of `validate.rs`, run via `cargo nextest run`. There are no Hypothesis/pytest strategies here; the validator's input space (a `&[Action]` vec) is enumerated with hand-picked fixtures that partition the conflict classes â€” that is the idiomatic Rust shape for this contract and is NOT a paradigm bypass. EXEMPT FROM PYTHON-PBT PARADIGM: Rust workspace, no Hypothesis. The validator's correctness is exhaustively pinned by the fixture set (cross-route accepted; same-route same-slot rejected; proto dimension distinct). DES phase mapping within this step: PREPARE â€” re-read RCA Â§Root cause A + Â§Fix A1 and the architect doc prose; confirm the current tracker shapes in `validate.rs:159-162` (`xdp_keys`, `xdp_vips`, `cgroup_keys`, `cgroup_vips`) and the two cross-route arms at lines 187-194 (XDP-after-cgroup) and 221-228 (cgroup-after-XDP). RED_ACCEPTANCE â€” add `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_for_same_vip` (asserts `vec![update_service(1), register(vip_v4(1), 8080)]` is `Ok`) and `validate_accepts_xdp_and_cgroup_distinct_proto_same_vip_port` (XDP tcp/53 + cgroup udp/53 for one VIP is `Ok`); run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` and watch both fail. RED_UNIT â€” N/A (no new internal helper; the change is deletion + tracker simplification). GREEN â€” apply the logic deletion (use Serena `find_symbol` / `replace_symbol_body` on `validate_reconcile_output` to edit the match block and the tracker declarations; delete `service_write_key`'s `WriteRoute` plumbing only if it becomes unused â€” it does NOT, both routes still need keying). Apply the verbatim module-doc replacement (replace the `//!` header block). Delete the two `validate_rejects_*_for_same_vip` test fns; the acceptance test added in RED is their replacement. Run the default lane, then the integration `--no-run` typecheck, then dst-lint + clippy. COMMIT â€” `git commit` (no `--no-verify`), body lists the three changes (logic narrowing, verbatim doc, test flip) and cites ADR-0053 revision 2026-06-03 + RCA Â§Fix A1; `Step-ID: 01-01` trailer. POST-GREEN mutation run per `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ "Mutation testing is the exception" with `run_in_background: true`; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json`. Record phase transitions via `PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python3 -m des.cli.log_phase` â€” do NOT edit `execution-log.json` directly.

steps.name: Fix B â€” gate should_dispatch's None|Pending arm on the attempt-budget backoff window so a suppressed dispatch no longer spins every tick
steps.description: RED â†’ GREEN â†’ COMMIT in one step. Target: `crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs` (`should_dispatch`, currently lines 459-477). RED: add a unit test `mixed_backend_does_not_re_dispatch_within_backoff_window_with_no_status_row` in the existing `#[cfg(test)]` block. It calls `should_dispatch(actual_status = None, desired_fingerprint = FP, retry = Some(&RetryMemory { attempts: 1, last_failure_seen_at: T0, last_attempted_fingerprint: FP }), now = T0 + Duration::from_millis(500))` (i.e. inside the 1s backoff window, prior attempt at the SAME fingerprint, no status row) and asserts it returns `false` (do NOT dispatch â€” backoff window engaged). Against current code it returns `true` (the `None | Some(Pending) => true` arm fires unconditionally) â†’ RED. Add a companion test `mixed_backend_re_dispatches_immediately_on_fingerprint_change` asserting `should_dispatch(None, NEW_FP, Some(&RetryMemory { attempts: 1, last_failure_seen_at: T0, last_attempted_fingerprint: OLD_FP }), now = T0 + Duration::from_millis(1))` returns `true` (spec changed â†’ reset, dispatch now). Add `mixed_backend_re_dispatches_after_window_elapses` asserting the same-fingerprint case returns `true` once `now >= last_failure_seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(attempts)`. Watch the first fail. GREEN: (1) lift the `Failed`-arm gate expression `retry.is_none_or(|r| now >= r.last_failure_seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(r.attempts))` into a shared pure helper (e.g. `fn backoff_window_elapsed(retry: Option<&RetryMemory>, now: UnixInstant) -> bool`); call it from the `Failed` arm unchanged. (2) Change the `None | Some(ServiceHydrationStatus::Pending)` arm from `=> true` to: if `retry` shows prior attempts at the SAME `last_attempted_fingerprint` as `desired_fingerprint`, return `backoff_window_elapsed(retry, now)`; otherwise (no retry memory, OR `last_attempted_fingerprint != desired_fingerprint`) return `true` (dispatch immediately â€” fresh spec or never-attempted). NO `next_attempt_at` field is added â€” the gate recomputes `last_failure_seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(attempts)` from persisted inputs every call (honors 'Persist inputs, not derived state'). Confirm `RetryMemory` already carries `attempts`, `last_failure_seen_at`, and `last_attempted_fingerprint` (it does per RCA Â§Root cause B); if `last_attempted_fingerprint` is not yet a field, surface that as a blocker (it should already exist â€” the RCA states the inputs 'are already persisted in RetryMemory but never consulted in the None/Pending path'). COMMIT: single cohesive commit, `Step-ID: 01-02`, normal hooks.
steps.criteria: `should_dispatch`'s `None | Some(Pending)` arm consults the backoff window: with prior attempts at the same `last_attempted_fingerprint` and `now` inside the window it returns `false`; with `now` past the window it returns `true`; with a changed fingerprint OR no retry memory it returns `true` immediately. The `Failed`-arm gate is now expressed via the shared `backoff_window_elapsed` helper, called from both arms with identical semantics. No `next_attempt_at` field exists on `RetryMemory` (`git grep next_attempt_at crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs` returns nothing). The three new unit tests pass; `mixed_backend_does_not_re_dispatch_within_backoff_window_with_no_status_row` is the load-bearing REDâ†’GREEN pin. `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core` green; `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` exits 0; `cargo dst` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (`should_dispatch` stays pure over its `now: UnixInstant` parameter â€” no `Instant::now()`); `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on overdrive-core. Mutation gate: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs` â‰Ą80%. Commit carries `Step-ID: 01-02`; no `--no-verify`.
acceptance_criteria: RCA Â§Root cause B (closure) â€” the `None | Some(Pending)` arm no longer returns `true` unconditionally; when a prior attempt at the same fingerprint exists and the backoff window has not elapsed, it returns `false`, throttling the spin to ~1 Hz (the constant-1s `backoff_for_attempt` value, unchanged).

RCA Â§Fix B â€” the `Failed`-arm gate is lifted into a shared helper applied identically in the `None | Pending` arm; the arm dispatches immediately on `desired_fingerprint != last_attempted_fingerprint` (level-triggered reset on spec change, per the client-go workqueue precedent).

RCA Â§Regression test 2 (No-spin convergence, the B half) â€” `mixed_backend_does_not_re_dispatch_within_backoff_window_with_no_status_row` fails against current code (returns `true`) and passes after the fix (returns `false`); this is the pure-decision unit-level pin of the no-spin guarantee.

Persist inputs, not derived state (`.claude/rules/development.md`) â€” NO `next_attempt_at` field; the deadline is recomputed every call from `last_failure_seen_at + backoff_for_attempt(attempts)`; only inputs `RetryMemory` already persists are consulted.

Out of scope honored â€” `backoff_for_attempt` stays constant 1s (exponential backoff is GH #137, NOT touched); the `RetryMemory`â†’runtime re-enqueue wiring (GH #160) is touched ONLY if Fix B strictly requires it â€” if `should_dispatch`'s caller already threads `RetryMemory` in, no #160 work is needed; if it does not and the gate cannot fire without it, surface to the user as a blocker (do NOT silently expand into #160's scope).

Reconciler purity preserved â€” `should_dispatch` and `backoff_window_elapsed` are pure over `(actual_status, desired_fingerprint, retry, now)`; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (no `Instant::now()` / `SystemTime::now()`).
Single-cut migration â€” the helper extraction + the arm change land in one commit; no shadow predicate, no two-phase rollout.
`cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` green on overdrive-core; no new `.expect()` outside binary boundaries; no new `unsafe`.

Mutation gate â‰Ą80% on `service_map_hydrator.rs`; the three new tests kill the 'always-true' / 'always-false' / 'flipped comparator' / 'ignored fingerprint-change reset' mutation classes on the `None | Pending` arm.
Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (unit â€” default nextest lane, pure over `should_dispatch` typed inputs, no I/O, no sim adapters, no feature flag). Mutation scope: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs`.
quality_gates: RED verified at runtime BEFORE the fix: `mixed_backend_does_not_re_dispatch_within_backoff_window_with_no_status_row` fails (current `None | Some(Pending) => true` returns `true`; test expects `false`). Watch it fail, then land the fix.
After the fix: all three new unit tests pass; `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core` green.
`git grep -n 'next_attempt_at' crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs` returns nothing â€” no derived-state field introduced.

Confirm `RetryMemory` already carries `last_attempted_fingerprint` (per RCA Â§Root cause B). If it does NOT, STOP and surface to the user as a blocker before adding a field â€” adding persisted state may overlap GH #160.

`cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck) exits 0; full integration run inside Lima VM (or LVH on CI) exits 0; `cargo dst` exits 0.
`cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on overdrive-core.

Mutation gate (post-GREEN, `run_in_background: true`): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/reconcilers/service_map_hydrator.rs` â‰Ą80%. cargo-mutants restores the tree; do NOT run `git checkout -- crates/`.
Production edit precision: `git diff --name-only HEAD~1` shows exactly one file (`service_map_hydrator.rs`).
deliverables: Single cohesive commit landing the `backoff_window_elapsed` shared helper + the `None | Some(Pending)` arm gate, consulting only persisted `RetryMemory` inputs.

Three new unit tests (`mixed_backend_does_not_re_dispatch_within_backoff_window_with_no_status_row`, `mixed_backend_re_dispatches_immediately_on_fingerprint_change`, `mixed_backend_re_dispatches_after_window_elapses`) transition RED â†’ GREEN within this commit.
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Rust workspace â€” unit tests are `#[test]` functions in the `#[cfg(test)]` block of `service_map_hydrator.rs`, run via `cargo nextest run`. EXEMPT FROM PYTHON-PBT PARADIGM: no Hypothesis in Rust. `should_dispatch` is a pure decision function; its input space (the `actual_status` sum type Ă— fingerprint match/mismatch Ă— retry presence Ă— now-vs-window) is partitioned by hand-picked fixtures that cover every arm â€” the idiomatic Rust shape. DES phase mapping: PREPARE â€” re-read RCA Â§Root cause B + Â§Fix B; read `should_dispatch` (lines 459-477) and locate `backoff_for_attempt` + the `RetryMemory` struct definition (confirm `attempts`, `last_failure_seen_at`, `last_attempted_fingerprint` fields exist â€” the RCA asserts they do). RED_ACCEPTANCE â€” write the three unit tests against `should_dispatch`; watch the within-window test fail. RED_UNIT â€” extract `backoff_window_elapsed(retry, now)` as the shared helper (it is the unit-level seam; a focused unit test on the helper itself is optional since the three `should_dispatch` tests exercise it transitively). GREEN â€” use Serena `replace_symbol_body` on `should_dispatch`: introduce the helper, replace the `None | Some(Pending) => true` arm with the fingerprint-aware gate. Keep the `Failed` arm calling the same helper. Run the default lane, integration `--no-run`, dst-lint, clippy. COMMIT â€” `git commit` (no `--no-verify`), body cites RCA Â§Fix B + the client-go workqueue precedent + 'Persist inputs, not derived state'; `Step-ID: 01-02` trailer. POST-GREEN mutation run with `run_in_background: true`; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json`. Record phase transitions via `PYTHONPATH=$HOME/.claude/lib/python python3 -m des.cli.log_phase`.

steps.name: Fix C part 1 â€” mint the conflict observation-row type, VersionedEnvelope, discriminant-offset const, and golden-bytes fixture in overdrive-core
steps.description: RED â†’ GREEN â†’ COMMIT in one step (schema-evolution atomic landing). Targets: `crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` (new type + envelope) and `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/<row>.rs` (new golden fixture) + `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution.rs` (mod registration). Define a new observation row capturing a genuine same-slot reconcile-output conflict (the surviving violation class after Fix A1). Suggested name `ReconcileConflictRow` (crafter may align with the existing observation-row naming convention in the file). Fields: enough to make the conflict queryable â€” e.g. `reconciler: ReconcilerName`, `target: TargetResource` (or the `ServiceId`/`vip` the conflict is keyed on), the conflicting `(vip, port, proto)` slot, the two `WriteRoute`s, and an `updated_at: LogicalTimestamp` (LWW ordering, mirroring `ServiceHydrationResultRowV1::updated_at`). Mirror `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope` (`observation_store.rs:782-863`) EXACTLY for the envelope machinery: payload struct `...V1`, enum `...Envelope { V1(...) }`, `pub type ...Latest = ...V1`, `impl VersionedEnvelope` with `type Latest`, `latest()`, `into_latest()`, `discriminant_offset_from_end()` (empirically pin via the regeneration aid â€” measure the offset for THIS payload's trailing footprint, do NOT copy 80 blindly), `known_discriminants() -> &[0]`, `type_name()`. RED: add the golden-fixture test file `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/reconcile_conflict_row.rs` mirroring `service_hydration_result_row.rs` (canonical V1 payload fn, `FIXTURE_V1` const placeholder, the `_v1_decodes_through_current_envelope` roundtrip test, the `_discriminant_offset_triangulation` test, the `_unknown_version_probe_surfaces` test, and the `#[ignore]`d `print_fixture_v1_bytes` regeneration aid). Register `mod reconcile_conflict_row;` in the `mod schema_evolution { ... }` block of `tests/schema_evolution.rs` (alphabetical position). The fixture test fails until the type compiles and FIXTURE_V1 is pinned â†’ RED. GREEN: land the type + envelope; run the `#[ignore]`d `print_fixture_v1_bytes` once (`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test schema_evolution -E 'test(/print_fixture_v1_bytes/)' --no-capture`), paste the emitted hex into `FIXTURE_V1` verbatim, re-pin `GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1` and `discriminant_offset_from_end()` to the measured value; the roundtrip + triangulation + unknown-version tests go GREEN. COMMIT: single atomic commit (type + envelope + fixture together per the Version-bump procedure), `Step-ID: 01-03`, normal hooks. NO consumer wiring in this step â€” `run_convergence_tick` is wired in 01-04.
steps.criteria: A new conflict observation-row type + `VersionedEnvelope` enum + `Latest`/payload aliases + `discriminant_offset_from_end()` const + `known_discriminants()` + `type_name()` exist in `observation_store.rs`, mirroring `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope`. A golden-bytes fixture file `crates/overdrive-core/tests/schema_evolution/reconcile_conflict_row.rs` exists with a pinned `FIXTURE_V1`, registered in `tests/schema_evolution.rs`'s `mod schema_evolution` block. The roundtrip test (`*_v1_decodes_through_current_envelope`), the discriminant-offset triangulation test, and the unknown-version probe test all pass. `GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1` (in the fixture file) and `discriminant_offset_from_end()` (in the envelope impl) agree on the empirically-measured offset. `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test schema_evolution` green; `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on overdrive-core. No consumer (run_convergence_tick) wiring. Mutation gate: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` â‰Ą80% (or vacuous-pass if the new type's lines carry no mutable operators â€” the `into_latest`/`latest` mappings are the mutation surface). Commit carries `Step-ID: 01-03`; no `--no-verify`.
acceptance_criteria: RCA Â§Fix C (part 1) â€” a new observation row captures a genuine same-slot reconcile-output conflict; operators can query it rather than grep tracing logs. The TYPE + envelope land here; the write-path lands in 01-04.

rkyv schema-evolution discipline (`.claude/rules/development.md` Â§ 'rkyv schema evolution' + `.claude/rules/testing.md` Â§ 'Archive schema-evolution roundtrip') â€” the new row goes through a `VersionedEnvelope` enum with a `V1` variant, `Latest`/payload aliases, a `discriminant_offset_from_end()` const, and a golden-bytes fixture; the envelope + fixture land in ONE atomic commit.

The envelope machinery mirrors `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope` (`observation_store.rs:782-863`) exactly: `latest()`, `into_latest()`, `known_discriminants() -> &[0]`, `type_name()`, and an EMPIRICALLY-measured `discriminant_offset_from_end()` (NOT copied from the 80-byte ServiceHydration offset â€” measured for this payload's trailing footprint via the regeneration aid).

The golden fixture file mirrors `service_hydration_result_row.rs`: canonical V1 payload fn, pinned `FIXTURE_V1` (generated once via the `#[ignore]`d `print_fixture_v1_bytes` aid, then verbatim), the roundtrip test, the triangulation test, the unknown-version probe test. FIXTURE_V1 is the load-bearing artifact.
`GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1` (fixture file) and `discriminant_offset_from_end()` (envelope impl) triangulate to the same measured value; the canonical archive places the V1 tag (0) at that offset.

Layer-1 / Layer-2 envelope discipline preserved â€” the new `...Envelope` enum is `pub` but NOT re-exported from `lib.rs` (mirroring the existing envelopes' non-re-export); the inner payload is constructed by callers via the public payload alias; the dst_lint variant-construction clause is not tripped (no `...Envelope::V1(` literal outside the defining module's own impls).
NO consumer wiring in this step â€” `run_convergence_tick` is untouched; `git diff --name-only HEAD~1` shows only `observation_store.rs`, the new fixture file, and `tests/schema_evolution.rs`.
`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test schema_evolution` green; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (the new type is pure data, no banned APIs); `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.
Single atomic commit per the Version-bump procedure; no shadow type, no two-phase rollout.

Mutation gate â‰Ą80% (or vacuous-pass): the `into_latest`/`latest` mappings and the offset const are the mutation surface; the roundtrip + triangulation tests kill the 'wrong variant mapping' / 'wrong offset' mutations.
Commit message carries `Step-ID: 01-03` trailer; normal pre-commit hooks pass (no `--no-verify`).
steps.tier: Tier 1 (schema-evolution golden-bytes â€” default nextest lane, pure in-memory rkyv roundtrip, no I/O, no feature flag). Mutation scope: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs`.

After GREEN: `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test schema_evolution` green (roundtrip + triangulation + unknown-version probe all pass for the new row).

FIXTURE_V1 was generated via the `#[ignore]`d `print_fixture_v1_bytes` aid and pasted verbatim; `discriminant_offset_from_end()` and `GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1` were re-pinned to the MEASURED offset (not assumed 80).

The new envelope enum is NOT re-exported from `crates/overdrive-core/src/lib.rs` (`git grep -n 'ReconcileConflictRowEnvelope' crates/overdrive-core/src/lib.rs` returns nothing â€” Layer-1 discipline).

`cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-core` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck) exits 0; full integration run inside Lima VM (or LVH on CI) exits 0.
`cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (no banned-API on the new type; the dst_lint variant-construction clause is not tripped); `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on overdrive-core.

Mutation gate (post-GREEN, `run_in_background: true`): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --package overdrive-core --file crates/overdrive-core/src/traits/observation_store.rs` â‰Ą80% or vacuous-pass (no mutable operators on the new lines). cargo-mutants restores the tree; do NOT run `git checkout -- crates/`.
No consumer wiring: `git diff --name-only HEAD~1` shows only `observation_store.rs`, `tests/schema_evolution/reconcile_conflict_row.rs`, and `tests/schema_evolution.rs`.
deliverables: New conflict observation-row type + `VersionedEnvelope` enum + `Latest`/payload aliases + discriminant-offset const + `known_discriminants` + `type_name` in `observation_store.rs`, mirroring `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope`.

New golden-bytes fixture file `tests/schema_evolution/reconcile_conflict_row.rs` with pinned `FIXTURE_V1`, registered in `tests/schema_evolution.rs`; roundtrip + triangulation + unknown-version tests GREEN.
steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Rust workspace â€” the schema-evolution test is a golden-bytes roundtrip `#[test]`, run via `cargo nextest run`. EXEMPT FROM PYTHON-PBT PARADIGM: no Hypothesis in Rust; AND the golden-bytes fixture is a deliberate single-example pin (the whole point is to pin ONE canonical V1 byte layout â€” a property test would defeat the regression intent). Compensate: the discriminant-offset triangulation test + the unknown-version probe test cover the envelope's introspection surface. DES phase mapping: PREPARE â€” read `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope` (`observation_store.rs:782-863`) and its fixture (`tests/schema_evolution/service_hydration_result_row.rs`) as the verbatim template; read the `VersionedEnvelope` trait in `crates/overdrive-core/src/codec/`; confirm the row naming convention in `observation_store.rs` for the new type's name. RED_ACCEPTANCE â€” write the new fixture file mirroring `service_hydration_result_row.rs` with a placeholder `FIXTURE_V1 = ""`; register the mod; run the schema-evolution test and watch it fail (compile error on the missing type, then roundtrip failure on the empty fixture). RED_UNIT â€” N/A. GREEN â€” land the type + envelope in `observation_store.rs` (use Serena to insert adjacent to `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope`); run the `#[ignore]`d `print_fixture_v1_bytes` aid (`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-core --test schema_evolution -E 'test(/print_fixture_v1_bytes/)' --no-capture`); paste the hex into `FIXTURE_V1`; measure the discriminant offset (the triangulation test will tell you if the assumed value is wrong â€” adjust `discriminant_offset_from_end()` and `GOLDEN_DISCRIMINANT_OFFSET_V1` together until it passes). Run the schema-evolution suite, then dst-lint + clippy. COMMIT â€” `git commit` (no `--no-verify`), body cites RCA Â§Fix C + the rkyv schema-evolution Version-bump procedure + names `ServiceHydrationResultRowEnvelope` as the template; `Step-ID: 01-03` trailer. POST-GREEN mutation run with `run_in_background: true`. NOTE on known rkyv-envelope forward-traps (`feedback_rkyv_envelope_forward_traps`): the dst-lint alias bypass + the discriminant-offset re-pin discipline are the relevant ones here â€” re-pin the offset against THIS payload, do not inherit 80. steps.name: Fix C part 2 â€” wire run_convergence_tick to write the conflict observation row on validator Err, alongside the existing tracing event (surface-then-continue)
steps.description: SCOPE CORRECTION 2026-06-03 (decomposition gap found during the first 01-04 attempt, which logged BLOCKED_BY_DEPENDENCY and made no source change): step 01-03 landed the `ReconcileConflictRow` TYPE + `VersionedEnvelope` + golden fixture in overdrive-core, but NOT the observation TRANSPORT surface needed to write/read it. There is no `ObservationRow::ReconcileConflict` variant, no `ObservationStore` reader, and no `SimObservationStore`/`LocalObservationStore` apply+read for the row â€” so `run_convergence_tick` cannot write it and the acceptance test cannot query it. The transport surface is intrinsic to the user-approved 'queryable observation row' (Fix C), not new scope, so 01-04 is EXPANDED to land it (NOT a renumber â€” step-ID format `^\d{2}-\d{2}$` admits no id between 01-03 and 01-04, and re-running 01-04 cleanly appends EXECUTED over the prior BLOCKED entries). GREEN now has TWO parts: (Part 0, transport) add `ObservationRow::ReconcileConflict(ReconcileConflictRow)` to the enum (`observation_store.rs:516` neighbourhood), add an `ObservationStore` reader method mirroring the `service_hydration` reader, implement apply+read in `SimObservationStore` (`crates/overdrive-sim/src/adapters/observation_store.rs`) and envelope-wrapped LWW write+read in `LocalObservationStore` (`crates/overdrive-store-local/src/observation_backend.rs`) â€” all mirroring the `ServiceHydration` precedent; the row SCHEMA + golden fixture from 01-03 are UNTOUCHED. (Part 1, original 01-04) Target: `crates/overdrive-control-plane/src/reconciler_runtime.rs` (the `if let Err(violation)` arm at lines 1140-1149). RED: add an acceptance test `genuine_same_slot_conflict_produces_queryable_observation_row` in the overdrive-control-plane acceptance lane (NEW file `tests/acceptance/reconcile_conflict_observation.rs`, registered in `tests/acceptance.rs`'s `mod acceptance` block, OR appended to an existing convergence-tick acceptance file â€” crafter's call, prefer a focused new file). The test drives `run_convergence_tick` against sim adapters with a reconciler/fixture that emits a GENUINE same-slot conflict (two XDP writes to one `(vip, port, proto)` slot, or two cgroup writes to one slot â€” the surviving violation class after Fix A1; the simplest path is a hand-built `Vec<Action>` fed through a tiny stub reconciler, or directly asserting the runtime arm via the smallest fixture that reaches the validator `Err` branch). It asserts: (a) the conflict observation row is queryable via `state.obs` after the tick (read it back and assert the slot/routes match), AND (b) dispatch was skipped (no `DataplaneUpdateService`/`RegisterLocalBackend` side effect landed â€” assert the dataplane/obs shows no service write for that VIP this tick), AND (c) the convergence tick did NOT error/stop (it returned the normal result, View persisted). Against current code (b)+(c) hold but (a) FAILS â€” no row is written, only a tracing event â†’ RED. GREEN: in the `if let Err(violation)` arm, ADD a write of the new conflict observation row through `state.obs` (construct the payload from the `violation`'s structural fields â€” the `(vip, port, proto)` slot + the two `WriteRoute`s + a `LogicalTimestamp` of `(tick.tick + 1, node_id)` matching the action-shim's LWW convention), KEEPING the existing `tracing::error!` `reconciler.output.invariant_violation` event unchanged. Do NOT add a stop/return; the arm still skips dispatch and falls through to the View persist + yield. Also apply the architect doc's `## Note on Fix C` amendment: extend the 'Fail-safe semantics' paragraph in `validate.rs`'s module-doc to name the observation row as the queryable surface alongside the tracing event. COMMIT: single cohesive commit, `Step-ID: 01-04`, normal hooks.
steps.criteria: `run_convergence_tick`'s `if let Err(violation)` arm writes the new conflict observation row through `state.obs` AND keeps the existing `tracing::error!` `reconciler.output.invariant_violation` event; it does NOT stop/return early; dispatch is still skipped; the View still persists. The acceptance test `genuine_same_slot_conflict_produces_queryable_observation_row` asserts the row is queryable, dispatch was skipped, and the tick did not error. The `validate.rs` 'Fail-safe semantics' module-doc paragraph is extended (per the architect doc `## Note on Fix C`) to name the observation row as the queryable surface. `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-control-plane` green; `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-control-plane` exits 0; full integration run inside Lima VM (or LVH on CI) green; `cargo dst` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0; `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean. Posture preserved (`.claude/rules/reconcilers.md` self-heal) â€” NO `TerminalError` / controller-runtime stop-semantics imported; the tick returns its normal result; convergence retries next tick; the appliance OS self-heals.
The Kubernetes Events model is honored â€” the tracing event stays as the best-effort human signal; the observation row is the machine-queryable control signal. Both fire on a genuine conflict.

steps.implementation_notes: TEST PARADIGM (mandatory): Rust workspace â€” the regression test is an acceptance `#[tokio::test]` driving `run_convergence_tick` against sim adapters, run via `cargo nextest run`. EXEMPT FROM PYTHON-PBT PARADIGM: no Hypothesis in Rust; AND this is a wiring/integration test (verifies the obs-write side effect of the validator-Err arm), which per the standing paradigm mandate REMAINS single-example. DES phase mapping: PREPARE â€” re-read RCA Â§Fix C + Â§Posture; read the `if let Err(violation)` arm (`reconciler_runtime.rs:1140-1168`) and the action-shim's `obs.write` + LWW `LogicalTimestamp` convention (search for `tick.tick + 1` in the dispatch path); read an existing overdrive-control-plane acceptance test (e.g. the runtime-convergence acceptance shape) for the sim-adapter fixture pattern (`Arc<SimClock>`, `Arc<SimObservationStore>`, `Arc<LocalIntentStore>` on a TempDir, `AppState`). Confirm 01-01 and 01-03 are landed (the post-A1 conflict surface + the row type). RED_ACCEPTANCE â€” write the new acceptance test; the simplest fixture that reaches the validator `Err` arm is a small stub reconciler (or a direct `Vec<Action>` with two same-slot writes routed through the tick); assert (a) row queryable via `state.obs`, (b) dispatch skipped, (c) tick returned normally. Watch (a) fail. RED_UNIT â€” N/A. GREEN â€” in the `Err(violation)` arm, construct the conflict row payload from the `violation`'s structural fields and `state.obs.write(...)` it (use the `LogicalTimestamp { counter: tick.tick + 1, writer: state.node_id.clone() }` shape); keep the `tracing::error!` unchanged; do NOT add a `return`/stop. Apply the `validate.rs` 'Fail-safe semantics' doc amendment (extend the paragraph to name the observation row). Run the default lane, then dst, then the integration `--no-run` typecheck + full Lima run, then dst-lint + clippy. POST-GREEN mutation run with `run_in_background: true` (via `cargo xtask lima run --` on macOS if integration tests participate).
